organizations.org: Moms Demand Action for Gun Sense in America — Texas chapter
organizations.notes: Nadia Barbot is an Austin technology product manager (Group Product Manager at Sysco LABS; formerly Bazaarvoice) who serves as Secretary of the Pecan Springs-Springdale Hills Neighborhood Association — the association covering the 78723 Mueller-area zip on the filing, which corroborates the identity directly. Her heavy federal donation history (77 contributions, lean 1.0) is consistent with her documented gun-safety advocacy; no individual PAC-level records were retrievable through public search tools, and no TEC lobbyist registration was found.

organizations.org: Mitchell Family Corporation / Mitchell Historic Properties
organizations.notes: Sheridan Mitchell Lorenz, Austin TX 78703 — corroborated by FEC individual-contribution records listing 'Sheridan Lorenz, Austin TX 78703, employer Mitchell Family Corp.' She is a daughter of Cynthia and George P. Mitchell (Mitchell Energy & Development; credited with pioneering commercial shale-gas extraction), past vice president of the Mitchell Family Corporation and of Mitchell Historic Properties (Galveston real estate), president/director of G-1 Corporation, VP and managing member of Circle Ten Inc., and a director of Mitchell Island Investments. She is a board member of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation, which funds climate-change, renewable-energy, aquifer-protection and land-conservation work. Her personal wealth derives from an oil-and-gas fortune, but no public record shows her holding an executive, board, or ownership role in an operating oil & gas company, so no oil_gas flag is recorded.

organizations.role: Member, Real Estate - Commercial directory listing
organizations.notes: Perry Lorenz is a 50-year Austin resident who has worked primarily as a principal in the purchase, sale, development and management of downtown Austin real estate since 1977, forming and serving as managing general partner of numerous downtown development partnerships. With partners he has developed 1,000+ condominium units in mixed-use projects including Spring, BartonPlace, Este, The Pedernales, Saltillo Lofts and The Independent, and controlled full city blocks where several of downtown Austin's tallest towers now stand (including the site of the 1,022-ft Waterline). His Austin Chamber listing (Real Estate - Commercial, 1311-A E. Sixth St.) matches the East Austin office described in coverage of the Mitchell family. Zip 78703 (Old West Austin/Clarksville) is consistent. The filing's employer 'Mitchell Family Corp' is corroborated: Lorenz is the husband of Sheridan Mitchell Lorenz, daughter of the late Texas oil and gas billionaire George P. Mitchell, and father of Katherine Lorenz, president of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation; Mitchell Family Corp is the family's Austin/The Woodlands family office. Note: Mitchell Family Corp is a family office and philanthropic-support entity, not an operating oil & gas company (Mitchell Energy & Development was sold to Devon Energy in 2002), so no oil_gas flag is asserted for Lorenz himself.

organizations.org: Greater Austin Hispanic Chamber of Commerce
organizations.notes: Raquel Valdez Sanchez is President & CEO of BCL of Texas, a statewide nonprofit CDFI (community development lender) headquartered in Austin; she was named CEO in September 2024 after serving as COO. Filed occupation 'CEO' and employer 'BCL of Texas' match the public record exactly, and she is an Austin resident serving on a City of Austin commission, consistent with the 78745 Austin zip.

organizations.role: Past President; first recipient (2003) and namesake of the John V. Nyfeler, FAIA Community Service Award
organizations.notes: John V. Nyfeler, FAIA is an Austin consulting architect (UT-Austin B.Arch), 40+ years in commercial/institutional architecture, with his practice at 1805 Adriane Drive, Unit B, Austin, TX 78721 — an East Austin address inside Travis County Commissioner Precinct 1, the seat held by the donee Jeff Travillion. Identity is corroborated by FEC individual-contribution records listing 'JOHN NYFELER (THE NYFELER ORGANIZATION/ARCHITECT), zip 78721' giving to End Citizens United, matching both the 78721 address and the donor's all-Democratic federal giving pattern (lean 1.0). He is a past president of AIA Austin and of the Texas Society of Architects, served on the AIA National Board of Directors 2012-2014, and AIA Austin's community-service award is named for him as its first recipient. Note: a separate FEC mirror row for an ARCHIPAC contribution lists zip 78705 for the same employer string — both are the same practitioner across different years/addresses, but the zips differ between records.

organizations.org: Siero Media (Sissy Siero Voiceover & Media LLC)
organizations.notes: Sissy Siero is Founder & CEO of Siero Media, a corporate multimedia/podcast production agency at 3005 S Lamar Blvd, Austin (inside the 78704 zip on the filing); she launched the firm in 2020 after roughly 18 years in multimedia production. Occupation, employer and location on the donation record all match. Certified WBE/LGBTE/SWOB/HUB business. No TEC lobbyist registration; no PAC or advocacy-org tie on any side of the searched spectrums.

Sissy Siero is Founder & CEO of Siero Media, a corporate multimedia/podcast production agency at 3005 S Lamar Blvd, Austin (inside the 78704 zip on the filing); she launched the firm in 2020 after roughly 18 years in multimedia production. Occupation, employer and location on the donation record all match. Certified WBE/LGBTE/SWOB/HUB business. No TEC lobbyist registration; no PAC or advocacy-org tie on any side of the searched spectrums.

Reported occupation 'Director' and employer 'PODER' match exactly a well-documented East Austin public-record figure: Susana Almanza co-founded PODER in 1991 and serves as its director. She is a longtime environmental-justice organizer, a two-time Austin City Council District 3 candidate (2014, 2018), and a 2021 appointee to the National Environmental Justice Advisory Council. Her documented advocacy is environmental justice, anti-gentrification and land use in East Austin; no federal PAC, lobbyist-registry, or Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas, or defense-industry affiliations were found on either side of those spectrums.

organizations.org: City of Austin 2018 Charter Review Commission
organizations.notes: Confirmed via People's Community Clinic's own management-team page: Joy Authur is Chief Development & Communications Officer at People's Community Clinic, a nonprofit safety-net health center in Austin — an exact match to the filing's 'Chief Dev Officer / Peoples Community Clinic'. The surname is correctly spelled 'Authur', not 'Arthur'; searches on both spellings resolved to the same person. She was previously a sales and marketing executive in the private sector and holds a History degree from UT Austin. She has a documented civic and city-government footprint (below). FEC aggregate for this donor is 28 contributions with a fully Democratic-side partisan lean; no ideological PAC (AIPAC/UDP, J Street, NRA-affiliated, oil & gas, or defense-contractor) recipient surfaced in public search. Note: contribution-level FEC recipient detail could not be pulled directly because the FEC public API rate limit was exhausted during this run.

organizations.org: Southwest Network for Environmental and Economic Justice
organizations.role: Helped found; co-chair of the Coordinating Council
organizations.notes: Reported occupation 'Director' and employer 'PODER' match exactly a well-documented East Austin public-record figure: Susana Almanza co-founded PODER in 1991 and serves as its director. She is a longtime environmental-justice organizer, a two-time Austin City Council District 3 candidate (2014, 2018), and a 2021 appointee to the National Environmental Justice Advisory Council. Her documented advocacy is environmental justice, anti-gentrification and land use in East Austin; no federal PAC, lobbyist-registry, or Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas, or defense-industry affiliations were found on either side of those spectrums.

Confirmed via People's Community Clinic's own management-team page: Joy Authur is Chief Development & Communications Officer at People's Community Clinic, a nonprofit safety-net health center in Austin — an exact match to the filing's 'Chief Dev Officer / Peoples Community Clinic'. The surname is correctly spelled 'Authur', not 'Arthur'; searches on both spellings resolved to the same person. She was previously a sales and marketing executive in the private sector and holds a History degree from UT Austin. She has a documented civic and city-government footprint (below). FEC aggregate for this donor is 28 contributions with a fully Democratic-side partisan lean; no ideological PAC (AIPAC/UDP, J Street, NRA-affiliated, oil & gas, or defense-contractor) recipient surfaced in public search. Note: contribution-level FEC recipient detail could not be pulled directly because the FEC public API rate limit was exhausted during this run.

Nadia Barbot is an Austin technology product manager (Group Product Manager at Sysco LABS; formerly Bazaarvoice) who serves as Secretary of the Pecan Springs-Springdale Hills Neighborhood Association — the association covering the 78723 Mueller-area zip on the filing, which corroborates the identity directly. Her heavy federal donation history (77 contributions, lean 1.0) is consistent with her documented gun-safety advocacy; no individual PAC-level records were retrievable through public search tools, and no TEC lobbyist registration was found.

Kellee Coleman is a City of Austin Equity Office staffer, matching the filing's 'COA' employer string, 'Manager' occupation, and Austin location; she is listed by name and department on the AFSCME Local 1624 executive board roster as a City Vice President — Local 1624 represents City of Austin and Travis County employees. Zip 78748 could not be independently confirmed and more than one Kellee Coleman appears in Austin-area search results, so identity rests on name + City of Austin employment rather than address. A secondary claim of Dell Medical School Community Strategy Team membership surfaced only via a LinkedIn profile that could not be read directly (HTTP 999), so it is not recorded as an affiliation. No FEC contribution record (pipeline count 0), no TEC lobbyist registration, and no pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, oil & gas, gun-rights, gun-control, or defense-contractor ties found.

organizations.role: Former longtime member and past chairman
organizations.notes: Perry Lorenz is a 50-year Austin resident who has worked primarily as a principal in the purchase, sale, development and management of downtown Austin real estate since 1977, forming and serving as managing general partner of numerous downtown development partnerships. With partners he has developed 1,000+ condominium units in mixed-use projects including Spring, BartonPlace, Este, The Pedernales, Saltillo Lofts and The Independent, and controlled full city blocks where several of downtown Austin's tallest towers now stand (including the site of the 1,022-ft Waterline). His Austin Chamber listing (Real Estate - Commercial, 1311-A E. Sixth St.) matches the East Austin office described in coverage of the Mitchell family. Zip 78703 (Old West Austin/Clarksville) is consistent. The filing's employer 'Mitchell Family Corp' is corroborated: Lorenz is the husband of Sheridan Mitchell Lorenz, daughter of the late Texas oil and gas billionaire George P. Mitchell, and father of Katherine Lorenz, president of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation; Mitchell Family Corp is the family's Austin/The Woodlands family office. Note: Mitchell Family Corp is a family office and philanthropic-support entity, not an operating oil & gas company (Mitchell Energy & Development was sold to Devon Energy in 2002), so no oil_gas flag is asserted for Lorenz himself.

Sheridan Mitchell Lorenz, Austin TX 78703 — corroborated by FEC individual-contribution records listing 'Sheridan Lorenz, Austin TX 78703, employer Mitchell Family Corp.' She is a daughter of Cynthia and George P. Mitchell (Mitchell Energy & Development; credited with pioneering commercial shale-gas extraction), past vice president of the Mitchell Family Corporation and of Mitchell Historic Properties (Galveston real estate), president/director of G-1 Corporation, VP and managing member of Circle Ten Inc., and a director of Mitchell Island Investments. She is a board member of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation, which funds climate-change, renewable-energy, aquifer-protection and land-conservation work. Her personal wealth derives from an oil-and-gas fortune, but no public record shows her holding an executive, board, or ownership role in an operating oil & gas company, so no oil_gas flag is recorded.

organizations.org: KOOP Radio 91.7 FM (Austin community radio, nonprofit)
organizations.role: Programming Committee member representing News and Public Affairs programmers; volunteer producer and co-editor of the station's 'Democracy Now!' broadcast
organizations.notes: Michelle Manning-Scott moved to Austin in 2018, studied mass communications in Boston and Fort Worth, and has been on KOOP Radio's volunteer production team since early 2019. KOOP's own profile of her states she and Ken McKenzie-Grant 'edit and air the syndicated Democracy Now! show, 2-3pm on Thursdays' - directly matching the filing's occupation 'Editor' and employer 'Democracy Now!' plus her Austin 78757 address. She was associate producer on KOOP's 'Civil Rights and Wrongs' and produces station promos and PSAs. Her 12 federal donations with a fully Democratic lean are consistent with the small-dollar progressive giving pattern described in her profile; no ideological or industry PAC ties surfaced.

organizations.org: Ending Chronic Homelessness in Our Community (ECHO), Austin
organizations.notes: John V. Nyfeler, FAIA is an Austin consulting architect (UT-Austin B.Arch), 40+ years in commercial/institutional architecture, with his practice at 1805 Adriane Drive, Unit B, Austin, TX 78721 — an East Austin address inside Travis County Commissioner Precinct 1, the seat held by the donee Jeff Travillion. Identity is corroborated by FEC individual-contribution records listing 'JOHN NYFELER (THE NYFELER ORGANIZATION/ARCHITECT), zip 78721' giving to End Citizens United, matching both the 78721 address and the donor's all-Democratic federal giving pattern (lean 1.0). He is a past president of AIA Austin and of the Texas Society of Architects, served on the AIA National Board of Directors 2012-2014, and AIA Austin's community-service award is named for him as its first recipient. Note: a separate FEC mirror row for an ARCHIPAC contribution lists zip 78705 for the same employer string — both are the same practitioner across different years/addresses, but the zips differ between records.

organizations.org: City of Austin Parks and Recreation Board
organizations.role: Chair (former); board member across multiple terms
organizations.notes: Corroborated as Jane H. Rivera, PhD, decades-long Rosewood/East Austin neighborhood activist (78702 is Rosewood/East Austin) and co-author with Gilberto C. Rivera of 'Austin's Rosewood Neighborhood'; she chaired the City of Austin Parks and Recreation Board (documented in Austin Monitor coverage and PARD board records 2008-2019). Filing occupation 'Not Employed' is consistent with a retired civic figure; identity rests on name + East Austin ZIP + civic-donor pattern rather than a direct record match, hence medium.

organizations.org: Undoing Racism Austin / Undoing White Supremacy Austin
organizations.role: Founder and organizer; trainer with the People's Institute for Survival and Beyond
organizations.notes: D. Lauren Ross, Ph.D., P.E. is the owner of Glenrose Engineering, a woman-owned environmental engineering consultancy at 1405 Hillmont St, Austin TX 78704 — the firm address zip matches the donor zip 78704 exactly, and the occupation/employer strings ('Engineer' / 'Glenrose Engineering') match the firm. Her practice is water-quality protection, groundwater transport, solid waste and environmental litigation. Long public record of Austin environmental advocacy: she provided the technical expertise behind the Save Our Springs ordinance, worked on closure of the Gibraltar hazardous waste site, and opposed the Longhorn oil pipeline. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found and no ideological PAC giving surfaced in FEC searches (pipeline records 34 federal donations, fully Democratic-leaning, but no PAC-level detail was retrievable — the FEC data browser is a JS app and the open FEC API returned HTTP 429).

organizations.role: Member, Board of Directors — Southwest Key is the large Austin-headquartered nonprofit known for operating immigrant-youth shelters and charter schools
organizations.notes: FEC records for ROSA SANTIS of Austin, TX 78703 self-report occupation 'President' / 'CEO' at 'PEDRO SS SERVICES, INC.' (also abbreviated 'PSS INC') across 2008, 2012 and 2021 filings — an exact match to the donor record's employer string 'Pedro SS Services, Inc.', occupation 'President', and 78703 zip. Dun & Bradstreet places S S Pedro Services Inc in the power-line and telecommunications infrastructure construction contractors industry at 403 Springdale Rd, Austin, and FMCSA's SAFER database confirms the same company as an Austin-based private intrastate carrier. She holds a B.A. in Government and an M.S. in Political Science from UT Austin (magna cum laude) and serves on the board of directors of Southwest Key Programs. Gifts went to Kirk Watson (city) and Jeff Travillion (Travis County Pct 1), 2017-2022.

organizations.org: Texas Campaign for the Environment / TCE Fund
organizations.notes: Austin 78704 and the filing's 'Not Employed' match Robin Schneider, the longtime Executive Director of Texas Campaign for the Environment, who retired in April 2025 after roughly 25 years leading the group's anti-pollution and recycling advocacy (per her organizational bio and a LinkedIn listing her as retired as of April 2025). FEC records for 'Robin Schneider, Austin, TX' with employer 'Texas Campaign for the Environment' and occupation 'Executive Director' shift to 'Not Employed' beginning in late 2025, consistent with that retirement date. A same-named Robin Schneider in Castroville, TX is a different person and not this donor.

organizations.org: City of Austin Economic Prosperity Commission
organizations.role: Vice Chair (District 3 appointee of Council Member Jose Velasquez), term Dec 14 2023 - Feb 28 2027
organizations.notes: Raquel Valdez Sanchez is President & CEO of BCL of Texas, a statewide nonprofit CDFI (community development lender) headquartered in Austin; she was named CEO in September 2024 after serving as COO. Filed occupation 'CEO' and employer 'BCL of Texas' match the public record exactly, and she is an Austin resident serving on a City of Austin commission, consistent with the 78745 Austin zip.
